nodes.name: deep_evaluators.py
nodes.summary: Pluggable deep evaluation backends for the OOP pipeline. Defines DeepEvaluator ABC, DeepEvaluation dataclass, and TradingAgentsDeepEvaluator concrete implementation that wraps external/TradingAgents LangGraph multi-agent LLM debate for final stock decisions (BUY/SELL/HOLD). Injects API keys and installs news cache before graph execution.

nodes.name: DeepEvaluation
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding a single ticker's deep evaluation result: ticker, evaluation_date, agent_outputs dict, extra_outputs dict, and final_decision string.

nodes.name: DeepEvaluator
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for deep evaluators. Subclasses must define a non-empty `name` class attribute and implement `evaluate(tickers, ctx) -> list[DeepEvaluation]`.

nodes.name: TradingAgentsDeepEvaluator
nodes.summary: Concrete DeepEvaluator that wraps external/TradingAgents. Builds a LangGraph state graph per ticker, streams through multi-agent debate (bull/bear analysts, researcher, trader), extracts final BUY/SELL/HOLD decision. Handles tiktoken-based token counting for LLM context management.

nodes.name: _inject_api_keys
nodes.summary: Inject API keys (ALPHA_VANTAGE, OPENAI, ANTHROPIC, GOOGLE) into os.environ from pipeline config. Called before building the TradingAgents graph.

nodes.name: _install_news_cache
nodes.summary: Indirection seam that installs the Google News caching layer for TradingAgents. Monkeypatched by tests.

nodes.name: _build_graph
nodes.summary: Imports and builds the TradingAgents LangGraph state graph with LLM configuration and recursion limit.

nodes.name: _extract_decision
nodes.summary: Parses the final BUY/SELL/HOLD decision from the graph's final state. Returns default 'HOLD' if extraction fails.

nodes.name: TestDeepSeekReasonerStructuredOutput
nodes.summary: Tests structured output compatibility: deepseek-reasoner raises on with_structured_output, deepseek-v4 works normally. Methods: test_with_structured_output_raises_for_reasoner, test_with_structured_output_works_for_v4.

nodes.name: TestBaseClassIsolation
nodes.summary: Tests that base ChatOpenAI normalization does not propagate reasoning_content (isolation check). Methods: test_normalized_does_not_propagate_reasoning_content.

nodes.name: test_memory_log.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive tests for TradingAgents memory/log system: TradingMemoryLog CRUD, pending/resolved tracking, markdown log format, rating parsing from diverse LLM outputs, deferred reflection with stock return lookup, portfolio manager context injection, and legacy removal validation.

nodes.name: TestTradingMemoryLogCore
nodes.summary: Core trading memory log tests: file-based CRUD, idempotent storage, pending/resolved state tracking, markdown log format, rating parsing (Buy/Overweight/Hold) from diverse LLM outputs including bold markdown and numbered lists, past context retrieval with same/cross ticker filtering, and log rotation/pruning. 30 test methods.

nodes.name: TestDeferredReflection
nodes.summary: Tests for deferred reflection: batch update resolution, atomic file writes, reflection with stock return data via yfinance/SPY benchmark, edge cases (too-recent trades, delisted tickers, short histories), and resolve-entry marking. 14 test methods.

nodes.name: TestPortfolioManagerInjection
nodes.summary: Tests for portfolio manager past context injection: past trades appear in initial state, prompts include context sections, same-ticker prioritisation, cross-ticker reflection, context limits, and full store->resolve->inject lifecycle. 11 test methods.

nodes.name: TestLegacyRemoval
nodes.summary: Regression tests confirming legacy features are removed: financial_situation_memory, BM25, reflect_and_remember, and memory param on portfolio manager. Full pipeline regression check. 5 test methods.

nodes.name: default_config.py

nodes.name: trading_graph.py

nodes.name: conditional_logic.py

nodes.name: setup.py

nodes.name: __init__.py

nodes.name: StatsCallbackHandler

nodes.name: KeywordColorFormatter

nodes.name: ConditionalLogic
nodes.summary: Determines LangGraph conditional edge routing: should_continue_debate() alternates Bull/Bear researchers (2-round back-and-forth), should_continue_risk_analysis() rotates Aggressive/Conservative/Neutral analysts (3-round). Returns node names as routing targets.

nodes.name: GraphSetup
nodes.summary: Builds the complete LangGraph StateGraph: creates 10 agent nodes (Analyst Team with parallel execution, Bull/Bear Researchers, Research Manager, Trader, 3 Risk Analysts, Portfolio Manager) and wires all edges/conditional edges for the investment debate and risk analysis state machine.

nodes.name: Propagator
nodes.summary: Manages state lifecycle: create_initial_state() initializes AgentState with company_name, trade_date, empty analyst reports, InvestDebateState and RiskDebateState. get_graph_args() returns stream_mode='values' and recursion_limit config.

nodes.name: Reflector
nodes.summary: Performs post-trade reflection: reflect_on_final_decision() sends the final decision plus raw/alpha returns to the LLM, which returns 2-4 sentences analyzing directional correctness, thesis validity, and concrete lessons. Used in Phase B deferred reflection.

nodes.name: SignalProcessor
nodes.summary: Wraps parse_rating() to extract the 5-tier rating (Buy/Overweight/Hold/Underweight/Sell) from Portfolio Manager's rendered markdown output. Maintains backward-compatible LLM constructor argument.

nodes.name: TradingAgentsGraph
nodes.summary: Main orchestrator: initializes deep/quick thinking LLM clients, creates ToolNode with data tools, instantiates ConditionalLogic/GraphSetup/Propagator/Reflector/SignalProcessor, compiles the LangGraph workflow with optional SQLite checkpointing, runs analysis with deferred reflection (Phase B), YFinance return fetching, memory log integration, and tool call message filtering.

nodes.name: PortfolioRating
nodes.summary: Enum with 5 values: BUY, OVERWEIGHT, HOLD, UNDERWEIGHT, SELL. Used as the rating field type in PortfolioDecision.

nodes.name: ResearchManagerDecision
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseModel for Research Manager structured output: decision (Buy/Sell/Hold string with reasoning) plus reasoning_quote (exact excerpt from analyst reports).

nodes.name: TraderDecision
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseModel for Trader structured output: trade_plan (entry/exit/position sizing) and rationale (linked to Research Manager's analysis).

nodes.name: PortfolioDecision
nodes.summary: Pydantic BaseModel for Portfolio Manager structured output: rating (PortfolioRating enum), executive_summary (2-4 sentence action plan), investment_thesis (detailed reasoning), optional price_target and time_horizon. Rendered back to markdown via render_pm_decision().

nodes.summary: Static utility class for technical indicator computation: get_stock_stats() loads OHLCV data, wraps with stockstats, and returns indicator values (SMA, RSI, MACD, etc.) for a given symbol, indicator name, and date.

nodes.name: _PerAgentCallTracker

nodes.name: display_announcements

nodes.name: get_config

nodes.summary: Routes tool method calls to vendor-specific implementations using VENDOR_METHODS lookup table with fallback chain support. Accepts method name plus *args/**kwargs.

nodes.name: get_vendor

nodes.name: get_category_for_method

nodes.name: search_google_news
nodes.summary: Core Google Gemini integration: creates genai.Client, configures google_search tool, sets system_instruction as Senior Equity Research Analyst persona, calls gemini-3-flash-preview with GenerateContentConfig. Returns response.text.

nodes.name: get_google_news
nodes.summary: Queries ticker-specific financial news via Gemini web search for a given date range. Constructs search prompt and delegates to search_google_news().

nodes.name: get_global_news_google
nodes.summary: Retrieves macroeconomic/global news via Gemini web search with configurable look_back_days and article limit. Uses gemini-3-flash-preview with temperature=0.0.

nodes.name: get_fundamentals_google
nodes.summary: Fetches fundamental data (P/E, P/S, P/B, debt-to-equity, cash flow, revenue, earnings, market cap) via Gemini web search for a 6-month lookback window.

nodes.name: get_YFin_data_online
nodes.summary: Fetches OHLCV price data from Yahoo Finance for a given symbol and date range using yfinance library with yf_retry wrapper.

nodes.name: get_stock_stats_indicators_window
nodes.summary: Computes rolling technical indicators (SMA, RSI, MACD, etc.) from Yahoo Finance OHLCV data using stockstats for a configurable lookback window.

nodes.name: get_all_indicators_yfinance
nodes.summary: Bulk computation of all supported technical indicators from Yahoo Finance data, returning a dict of indicator:value pairs.

nodes.name: create_initial_state
nodes.summary: Creates the initial AgentState dict for LangGraph: company_of_interest, trade_date, past_context, empty analyst reports, initialized InvestDebateState and RiskDebateState.

nodes.name: reflect_on_final_decision
nodes.summary: LLM reflection call: sends final_decision, raw_return, alpha_return to quick_thinking_llm with log_reflection_prompt. Returns 2-4 sentence prose analysis of directional correctness and lessons learned.

nodes.name: render_pm_decision
nodes.summary: Converts PortfolioDecision Pydantic instance back to markdown with **Rating**, **Executive Summary**, **Investment Thesis**, optional **Price Target** and **Time Horizon** section headers.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Smoke test entry: parses provider arg, creates LLM client, instantiates ResearchManager/Trader/PortfolioManager agents with structured-output bindings, runs SignalProcessor, prints typed Pydantic instances and rendered markdown for each agent.

nodes.name: run_analysis
nodes.summary: Interactive CLI wizard: guides user through ticker, date, analysts, research depth, LLM provider, model selection, output language, and thinking configuration. Builds config dict and invokes TradingAgentsGraph with live Rich progress display.

nodes.name: save_report_to_disk
nodes.summary: Saves complete analysis report to disk: creates organized subfolder structure per ticker/date, writes markdown sections for market, fundamentals, sentiment, news, investment debate, risk analysis, trader plan, and final decision.

nodes.name: TradingAgents Graph Engine
nodes.summary: LangGraph-based multi-agent LLM financial trading framework. Core graph components (TradingAgentsGraph, GraphSetup, ConditionalLogic, Propagator, Reflector, SignalProcessor) build a state machine with 10 agent nodes: 4 parallel analysts (market, fundamentals, news, social media), Bull/Bear researcher debate loop, Research Manager synthesis, Trader plan generation, and 3-way risk debate (Aggressive/Conservative/Neutral) leading to Portfolio Manager final decision with structured Pydantic output.

nodes.name: TradingAgents Dataflows
nodes.summary: Multi-vendor data abstraction layer: routes tool calls (stock data, indicators, fundamentals, news) to Alpha Vantage, Yahoo Finance, or Google Gemini backends. VENDOR_METHODS lookup table with fallback chains. Category-level configuration with tool-level overrides via data_vendors/tool_vendors config keys.

nodes.name: TradingAgents CLI
nodes.summary: Command-line interface layer: Typer-based entry point (cli/main.py) with interactive wizard and report generation modes, Rich-powered live progress display, questionary-based prompts (cli/utils.py), announcement fetcher (cli/announcements.py), and LangChain callback-based stats tracking (cli/stats_handler.py).

nodes.name: Multi-Agent LLM Debate
nodes.summary: Architectural pattern where multiple LLM agents (analysts, researchers, risk debaters) produce independent analyses, then engage in structured debate loops before a final manager synthesizes the decision. Implemented via LangGraph StateGraph with conditional routing logic controlling debate rounds.

nodes.name: Structured Output Decision Pipeline
nodes.summary: Pattern where decision-making agents (Research Manager, Trader, Portfolio Manager) produce both prose reasoning and typed Pydantic output via provider-native structured-output modes (json_schema for OpenAI/xAI, response_schema for Gemini, tool-use for Anthropic). Rendered back to markdown for downstream consumption.

nodes.name: agent_utils.py
nodes.summary: Central tool aggregator for TradingAgents: re-exports all data tools (stock, indicators, fundamentals, news) from sub-modules and provides helpers for language instruction, instrument context, and message clearing.

nodes.name: get_language_instruction
nodes.summary: Returns a prompt instruction string for the configured output language. Returns empty string for English (no extra tokens), otherwise directs agent to respond in that language.

nodes.name: build_instrument_context
nodes.summary: Constructs a prompt snippet describing the exact ticker instrument, preserving exchange suffixes (.TO, .L, .HK, .T) for accurate tool calls.

nodes.name: create_msg_delete
nodes.summary: Factory returning a LangGraph node that clears all messages from state via RemoveMessage and adds a placeholder HumanMessage for Anthropic compatibility.

nodes.name: rating.py
nodes.summary: Shared 5-tier rating vocabulary (Buy/Overweight/Hold/Underweight/Sell) and a deterministic heuristic parser used by Research Manager, Portfolio Manager, and memory log.

nodes.name: parse_rating
nodes.summary: Heuristically extracts a 5-tier rating from prose text. Two-pass: first looks for explicit "Rating: X" label (markdown-tolerant regex), then falls back to first rating word found anywhere. Returns Title-cased string or default (Hold).

nodes.name: RATINGS_5_TIER
nodes.summary: Canonical 5-tier rating tuple: (Buy, Overweight, Hold, Underweight, Sell) — most bullish to most bearish.

nodes.name: news_data_tools.py
nodes.summary: LangChain tools for news data: get_news (with date-aware variant), get_global_news, get_insider_transactions. The date-aware factory auto-corrects LLM-hallucinated dates within 30-day tolerance.

nodes.name: _make_date_aware_get_news
nodes.summary: Factory that creates a date-validating get_news tool. Validates start_date/end_date are within 30 days of trade_date; auto-corrects hallucinated dates with a warning log. Returns a wrapped LangChain @tool.

nodes.name: get_news
nodes.summary: LangChain @tool: retrieves news data for a ticker from the configured news_data vendor. Backwards-compatible non-validating version; prefer _make_date_aware_get_news for production use.

nodes.name: get_global_news
nodes.summary: LangChain @tool: retrieves global macroeconomic news for a given date with configurable look-back days and result limit.

nodes.name: get_insider_transactions
nodes.summary: LangChain @tool: retrieves insider transaction data for a ticker from the configured news_data vendor.

nodes.name: agent_states.py
nodes.summary: TypedDict definitions for all LangGraph state objects: AgentState (top-level, extends MessagesState), InvestDebateState (bull/bear debate tracking), RiskDebateState (risk team debate tracking).

nodes.name: InvestDebateState
nodes.summary: TypedDict for the researcher debate state: tracks bull_history, bear_history, history, current_response, judge_decision (all Annotated[str]), and count (Annotated[int]).

nodes.name: RiskDebateState
nodes.summary: TypedDict for the risk management debate state: tracks aggressive/conservative/neutral history and responses, latest_speaker, judge_decision, and count.

nodes.name: AgentState
nodes.summary: Top-level LangGraph state (extends MessagesState): holds company_of_interest, trade_date, all four analyst reports (market/sentiment/news/fundamentals), investment_debate_state, investment_plan, trader_investment_plan, risk_debate_state, final_trade_decision, and past_context.

nodes.name: structured.py
nodes.summary: Shared helpers for invoking agents with structured output (Pydantic schema) and a graceful free-text fallback. Used by Portfolio Manager, Trader, and Research Manager for consistent error handling.

nodes.name: bind_structured
nodes.summary: Wraps an LLM with llm.with_structured_output(schema). Returns the wrapped LLM or None if the provider does not support structured output (NotImplementedError/AttributeError), logging a warning.

nodes.name: invoke_structured_or_freetext
nodes.summary: Runs structured LLM call and renders result to markdown via render callback. On any failure (malformed JSON, provider error), falls back to plain llm.invoke so the pipeline never blocks. Returns string content.

nodes.name: core_stock_tools.py
nodes.summary: LangChain tool wrapping stock price data retrieval (OHLCV) via the configured core_stock_apis vendor.

nodes.name: get_stock_data
nodes.summary: LangChain @tool: retrieves OHLCV price data for a ticker symbol within a date range from the configured core_stock_apis vendor via route_to_vendor.

nodes.name: technical_indicators_tools.py
nodes.summary: LangChain tools for technical indicator retrieval: get_all_indicators (batched, preferred) and get_indicators (single or comma-separated with split).

nodes.name: get_all_indicators
nodes.summary: LangChain @tool: retrieves multiple technical indicators in a single batched call. Normalizes indicator names (lower, strip). Preferred over repeated get_indicators calls to minimize API round-trips.

nodes.name: get_indicators
nodes.summary: LangChain @tool: retrieves one or more technical indicators for a ticker. Splits comma-separated indicator strings and processes each individually, catching ValueError per indicator so one failure does not block others.

nodes.name: market_analyst.py
nodes.summary: Market Analyst agent: selects up to 8 technical indicators from a catalog (SMA, EMA, MACD, RSI, Bollinger, ATR, VWMA), calls get_stock_data + get_indicators, and produces a market_report.

nodes.name: create_market_analyst
nodes.summary: Factory: creates a Market Analyst LangGraph node. Builds a ChatPromptTemplate with indicator catalog as system_message, binds tools (get_stock_data, get_indicators), invokes LLM chain, and writes market_report to state.

nodes.name: market_analyst_node
nodes.summary: Inner LangGraph node: builds prompt with full indicator catalog + language instruction, partials template with tool_names/current_date/instrument_context, invokes chain, and writes market_report (only populated when no tool_calls remain).

nodes.name: analyst_team.py
nodes.summary: Analyst Team orchestrator: runs market, social, news, and fundamentals analysts concurrently via asyncio.gather, each with independent tool-call loops, per-type configuration, and iteration limits.

nodes.name: create_analyst_team
nodes.summary: Factory: returns an async LangGraph node that runs all selected analysts in parallel via asyncio.gather. Each analyst runs an independent tool-call loop against a local copy of messages; report fields are written directly to state.

nodes.name: analyst_team_node
nodes.summary: Inner async LangGraph node: builds per-analyst chains with date-aware news tools, launches all analysts concurrently via asyncio.gather, collects results into state_update dict mapping report fields to report strings.

nodes.name: _run_analyst
nodes.summary: Core async analyst loop: invokes LLM chain with tool calls up to max_iter iterations. Executes tool calls via run_in_executor, appends ToolMessage responses, logs vendor info per call. Returns (report_field, report_string) on completion or empty string on failure.

nodes.name: _build_chain
nodes.summary: Builds an LLM chain for a given analyst type: selects tools from _ANALYST_TOOLS, replaces get_news with date-aware wrapper for social/news analysts, injects explicit date range into system message, assembles ChatPromptTemplate.

nodes.name: _news_date_range
nodes.summary: Returns (start_date, end_date) tuple for a 7-day news window ending at current_date.

nodes.name: _ANALYST_TOOLS
nodes.summary: Per-analyst tool mapping dict: market→[get_stock_data,get_all_indicators], social→[get_news], news→[get_news,get_global_news], fundamentals→[get_fundamentals,get_balance_sheet,get_cashflow,get_income_statement].

nodes.name: _REPORT_FIELDS
nodes.summary: Per-analyst state field mapping: market→market_report, social→sentiment_report, news→news_report, fundamentals→fundamentals_report.

nodes.name: _MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS
nodes.summary: Per-analyst iteration caps: market=5, social=3, news=4, fundamentals=6; default=5.

nodes.name: social_media_analyst.py
nodes.summary: Social Media Analyst agent: searches company news and social media discussions via get_news to produce a sentiment_report with actionable insights.

nodes.name: create_social_media_analyst
nodes.summary: Factory: creates a Social Media Analyst LangGraph node. Uses get_news to search company-specific news and social media, produces a sentiment_report.

nodes.name: social_media_analyst_node
nodes.summary: Inner LangGraph node: builds prompt for social media/news analysis with language instruction, binds get_news tool, invokes LLM chain, writes sentiment_report to state.

nodes.name: news_analyst.py
nodes.summary: News Analyst agent: analyzes company-specific and global macroeconomic news using date-validated get_news + get_global_news tools, with dual anti-hallucination safeguards.

nodes.name: create_news_analyst
nodes.summary: Factory: creates a News Analyst LangGraph node. Uses _make_date_aware_get_news wrapper and get_global_news. Explicitly injects correct start_date/end_date into the system prompt as a second anti-hallucination safeguard.

nodes.name: news_analyst_node
nodes.summary: Inner LangGraph node: computes 7-day news window, creates date-validated get_news, builds prompt with explicit date instructions, binds both tools, invokes LLM chain, writes news_report.

nodes.name: fundamentals_analyst.py
nodes.summary: Fundamentals Analyst agent: analyzes financial statements using four tools (get_fundamentals, get_balance_sheet, get_cashflow, get_income_statement) to produce a fundamentals_report.

nodes.name: create_fundamentals_analyst
nodes.summary: Factory: creates a Fundamentals Analyst LangGraph node. Uses get_fundamentals, get_balance_sheet, get_cashflow, get_income_statement to produce a fundamentals_report with markdown table summary.

nodes.name: fundamentals_analyst_node
nodes.summary: Inner LangGraph node: builds prompt instructing the LLM to use all four fundamental tools, partials template, invokes chain, writes fundamentals_report to state.

nodes.name: TradingAgentsGraph
nodes.summary: Main entry point class for TradingAgents. Wraps the LangGraph state machine. propagate(ticker, date) method runs the full agent pipeline and returns BUY/SELL/HOLD decision. Located in tradingagents/graph/trading_graph.py.

nodes.name: AgentState
nodes.summary: Main LangGraph state object for TradingAgents. Shared data bus carrying ticker, date, analyst reports, debate transcripts, trader decision, risk analysis results, and final trade decision across all graph nodes.

nodes.name: InvestDebateState
nodes.summary: LangGraph sub-state for the Bull vs Bear Researcher debate loop. Tracks current speaker, debate round count, and message history. Mutually recursive with research_manager_node until consensus or max rounds.

nodes.name: RiskDebateState
nodes.summary: LangGraph sub-state for the Risk Management debate loop. Tracks risky/safe/neutral arguments, debate rounds, and final risk assessment verdict.

nodes.name: AnalystTeam
nodes.summary: Parallelized analyst orchestration module (tradingagents/agents/analysts/analyst_team.py). Runs Market, Social Media, News, and Fundamentals analysts concurrently via ThreadPoolExecutor. Each analyst independently runs a tool-call loop (fetch data -> re-invoke). Fault-tolerant: individual analyst failures do not block others.

nodes.name: FinancialSituationMemory
nodes.summary: Memory system for TradingAgents agents. Each reasoning agent (Bull/Bear Researcher, Research Manager, Trader, Risk Judge) has a dedicated instance backed by an in-memory ChromaDB collection. Stores past analysis results for reflection and learning across runs.

nodes.name: DEFAULT_CONFIG
nodes.summary: Default configuration dictionary in tradingagents/default_config.py. Fields: llm_provider, deep_think_llm, quick_think_llm, max_debate_rounds, data_vendors (per category routing). Override by passing config dict to TradingAgentsGraph.

nodes.name: AgentPipeline
nodes.summary: AI Hedge Fund agent pipeline: Market Analyst -> Social Media Analyst -> News Analyst -> Fundamentals Analyst -> Bull/Bear Researchers -> Research Manager -> Trader -> Risk Judge(s) -> Portfolio Manager. Yields BUY/SELL/HOLD signal with confidence and reasoning.

nodes.name: Backtester
nodes.summary: AI Hedge Fund backtesting subsystem. Reuses live analysis pipeline per historical date. Date loop iterates over trading days, applies decisions to a portfolio model with cash/positions tracking, computes performance metrics (Sharpe, max drawdown, CAGR, win rate).

nodes.name: WebApp
nodes.summary: AI Hedge Fund web application: FastAPI backend (routes for running analysis, backtesting; services for analysis/backtesting/decision layers; SQLite database) + React/Vite frontend. Backend structured as routes/services/database layers.

nodes.name: WeightAllocation
nodes.summary: FinRL weight allocation module. Supports equal-weight and min-variance methods. Accepts multi-index DataFrame (rows=daily per ticker) or daily price data. Extensible via MLStockSelectionStrategy.allocate_weights() subclass override.

nodes.name: TradingCalendar
nodes.summary: FinRL trading calendar module for real trading day filtering. Uses pandas-market-calendars or exchange-calendars. Handles full-missing, endpoint-missing, and mid-gap data scenarios. Supports NYSE, NASDAQ, SSE, SZSE exchanges and holiday calendars.

nodes.name: V2QuantStack
nodes.summary: AI Hedge Fund v2 quantitative trading stack. Clean architecture: core lib (domain types), data layer, strategies, backtesting separated. Key design decisions: no config objects in core, Pandas DataFrames over dicts, v1 isolation. Data models: Symbol, Bar, Signal, Order, Position, Portfolio, Trade.

layers.name: Presentation
layers.description: Streamlit dashboard — single-file web app (~2200 lines) with Plotly charts. Renders stock data lookup, technical screener, ML pipeline results, strategy backtesting, live/paper trading, portfolio analysis. Primary user-facing surface.

layers.name: Pipeline Core
layers.description: Pipeline orchestration and infrastructure — 4-step OOP pipeline (data_update → stock_selection → fast_evaluation → deep_evaluation) with pluggable backends, PipelineStep ABC, StepContext/StepResult, FullPipeline CLI orchestrator, APScheduler daemon, news cache, MLBucketSelector. Backends: FinrlStockSelector, AIHedgeFundFastEvaluator, TradingAgentsDeepEvaluator.

layers.name: Ingestion ML
layers.description: Data ingestion and ML model — AlphaVantage/Yahoo fetcher implementations with token-bucket rate limiter, batch ingestion pipeline, technical indicators, symbol resolution. XGBoost per-stock prediction: LabelMethod subclasses, rolling 5-day feature builder, parallel training/inference, Alpha Vantage/Yahoo Finance dataflows from TradingAgents.

layers.name: Data Access
layers.description: Database and data access — SQLAlchemy 2.0 engine/session (MySQL via pymysql), 16-table ORM models (stocks, daily_prices, stock_indicators, quarterly_fundamentals, pipeline_runs, selected_stocks, fast_evaluation_conclusion/analysts, deep_evaluation, etc.), StockRepository, idempotent SQL migration runner, Alembic migrations from ai-hedge-fund.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project configuration — YAML config loader with dotted-path overrides, per-step pipeline config schemas (data_update, stock_selection, fast_evaluation, deep_evaluation), Docker Compose orchestration, pyproject.toml/pytest.ini, LLM provider catalog files (ollama_models.json, api_models.json), environment variable schemas.

layers.name: External AI Hedge Fund
layers.description: External submodule: AI Hedge Fund — multi-agent fund analysis system with LangGraph workflows. Python backend: analyst agents (Ben Graham, Bill Ackman, Cathie Wood, etc.), portfolio optimization, risk management, backtesting engine, FastAPI API routes. TypeScript/React frontend: shadcn/ui panels, ReactFlow node graphs, settings pages, custom hooks, data mapping layer.

layers.name: External TradingAgents
layers.description: External submodule: TradingAgents — multi-agent LLM debate for deep stock evaluation. Bull/Bear Researcher agents, Research Manager, Trader, Portfolio Manager. LangGraph state machine with conditional routing and checkpointing. Structured output (Pydantic schemas). Multi-provider LLM clients (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, xAI, Qwen, GLM). Alpha Vantage/Yahoo Finance/Google News dataflows.

layers.name: External FinRL
layers.description: External submodule: FinRL-Trading — sector-based ML portfolio selection from S&P 500. GICS sector universe manager, Ridge/Stacking ensemble per sector, backtest engine, data processor, trading calendar, factor engineering, adaptive rotation engine. Powers the stock_selection pipeline step via FinrlStockSelector backend.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Test suite and documentation — pipeline step tests (data_update, stock_selection, fast_evaluation, deep_evaluation), orchestrator/config/backends/migration tests, E2E dashboard test, TradingAgents integration tests. Markdown docs, architecture specs, Jupyter notebooks, PNG figures, CLAUDE.md project instructions.

tour.title: Entry Point: Streamlit Dashboard
tour.description: Start at the user-facing web app. src/app.py (~1560 lines) is a single-file Streamlit dashboard with Plotly charts. It routes to pages via sidebar selectbox: Overview, Stock Data (5 tabs: Lookup, Technical, Screener, Manager, Predictions), ML Pipeline, Strategy Backtesting, Live/Paper Trading, Portfolio Analysis, Settings. src/display.py is a thin helper shared by app.py. Understand this first — every other component feeds data that ultimately renders here.

tour.title: Configuration & System Setup
tour.description: All runtime behavior is driven by config.yaml, loaded by src/config.py (the ConfigLoader supports dotted-path CLI overrides like --set fast_evaluation.top_n=5). Per-step pipeline config namespaces: data_update, stock_selection, fast_evaluation, deep_evaluation. Backwards-compat mapping handles legacy finrl_pipeline/ai_hedge_fund top-level keys. Key settings: Alpha Vantage API key, database URL, LLM provider/model selections, per-step backend selections.

tour.title: Database & ORM Layer
tour.description: MySQL via SQLAlchemy 2.0 with pymysql driver. src/database.py creates the engine and session factory. src/models.py (~430 lines) defines 16 ORM tables: stocks, daily_prices, stock_indicators, technical_indicators (JSON blob), stock_snapshots, quarterly_fundamentals (70+ metrics), plus ML tables (sample_features, sample_labels, stock_predictions) and pipeline tables (pipeline_runs, selected_stocks, fast_evaluation_conclusion, fast_evaluation_analysts, deep_evaluation). StockRepository in repository.py encapsulates all DB queries. Migrations via idempotent SQL runner in migrations/.

tour.title: Pipeline Orchestration: The 4-Step Engine
tour.description: The heart of AIStock. src/full_pipeline.py is a thin CLI shim that delegates to src/pipeline/orchestrator.py (FullPipeline class). The pipeline has 4 sequential steps, each with pluggable backends: (1) data_update — fetches latest price/fundamental data via Alpha Vantage/Yahoo; (2) stock_selection — selects top stocks using FinRL sector-based ML; (3) fast_evaluation — multi-agent AI hedge fund analysis per stock; (4) deep_evaluation — LLM debate with bull/bear researchers per stock. Each step is a PipelineStep subclass implementing run(context) -> StepResult. The orchestrator shares a single pipeline_run_id across steps, writes per-step reports to reports/full_pipeline/<run_id>/, and stops on first failure. Resume with --resume-from <step> --run-id <N>. Backends are selected by <step>.backend config key and registered via a decorator-based REGISTRY.

tour.title: Data Ingestion & XGBoost ML Model
tour.description: Two data pipelines: (A) src/main.py is a CLI for fetching single-symbol data (Alpha Vantage or Yahoo) with --incremental mode. src/scheduler.py wraps the batch ingestion pipeline (src/ingestion/pipeline.py) via APScheduler for periodic updates. src/news_cache.py caches Google News data to avoid rate limits. Token-bucket rate limiter in src/fetcher/ enforces API quotas. (B) XGBoost per-stock prediction: src/model/labels.py defines LabelMethod subclasses (binary classification labels), src/model/feature_builder.py computes rolling 5-day window features from OHLCV + technical indicators, src/model/train.py trains in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor, src/model/inference.py produces stock_predictions. Model artifacts stored as pickle files in models/. This is the data source for the stock_selection pipeline step.

tour.title: FinRL Stock Selection Pipeline
tour.description: src/finrl_pipeline.py (~650 lines) orchestrates the sector-based ML portfolio selection. It imports from external/FinRL-Trading, which provides: S&P 500 universe management via GICS sectors, Ridge/Stacking ensemble ML per sector (FEATURE_COLS, SECTOR_TO_BUCKET), data fetching from AIStock DB (AIStockDBSource class added to FinRL's data_fetcher.py), and backtest engine with benchmark comparison (SPY/QQQ). The pipeline writes data/selection_report_YYYYMMDD.csv and backtest JSON. src/ml_bucket_selector.py wraps FinRL's run_bucket() as an OOP class. CRITICAL: sys.path hazard — FinRL's data_fetcher.py inserts its own path at sys.path[0], so finrl_pipeline.py must pre-import config, database, repository at module level before any FinRL imports.

tour.title: AI-Powered Evaluation: Hedge Fund & Trading Agents
tour.description: Steps 3 and 4 of the pipeline bring LLM-powered analysis. (Step 3) AIHedgeFundFastEvaluator wraps external/ai-hedge-fund in-process. This multi-agent system has 15+ analyst agents (Warren Buffett, Ben Graham, Cathie Wood, etc.) each producing an investment thesis. Agents run via LangGraph with a FastAPI backend and a React/TypeScript frontend (ReactFlow node graphs, shadcn/ui panels). Results stored in fast_evaluation_conclusion (consensus scores) and fast_evaluation_analysts (per-analyst opinions). (Step 4) TradingAgentsDeepEvaluator wraps external/TradingAgents — a LangGraph state machine where Bull/Bear Researcher agents debate a stock, a Research Manager adjudicates, a Trader produces execution plans, and a Portfolio Manager decides BUY/SELL/HOLD. Results stored in deep_evaluation table with per-agent text fields. Backend registered in src/pipeline/backends/deep_evaluators.py.
